name: Gay Valimont
subject: Bringing Leadership Back to Northwest Florida
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gay Valimont
body:
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/70756F655332783372636F454353396562687376694F485948735343712B763672586D4157707A676459726C636F6733786F6E2B526572766D6752546567474F/editor_images/Screenshot%202025-01-10%20at%208.23.20%E2%80%AFAM.png]
Over the past few weeks, I’ve had the privilege of connecting with so many of you —whether it was at Okaloosa DEC meetings or connecting with Pensacola Beach locals, every conversation has reinforced what I already knew: Northwest Florida deserves better than what we’re getting from Washington.
I’ve spoken with retirees worried about their Social Security, small business owners struggling with rising costs, farmers frustrated by shrinking subsidies, and families feeling the squeeze of a healthcare system that just isn’t working for them. And let’s not forget our military families—who are the backbone of this district—too often overlooked despite their sacrifices.
That’s why I’m running. Because we need leaders who show up, listen, and fight for YOU —not just the wealthy and well-connected. I need your support to make this a reality, and keep the ball rolling.
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/6F7161555A6147304F4872462B7077334A516D506D4368393137574C796F4E6D7958474877436B46656B4D39654871536E3265526F44376248502B4D4F49374F/editor_images/Screenshot%202025-03-01%20at%2011.40.35%E2%80%AFAM.png]
My priorities remain clear:
✅ Protect Social Security & Medicare —These are earned benefits, not bargaining chips.
✅ Support Farmers & Small Businesses —They are the foundation of our local economy, and they need resources to grow.
✅ Defend Our Coastline & Environment —Our economy and way of life depend on it.
✅ Expand Healthcare Access —No one should have to choose between medical care and putting food on the table.
I know our district’s potential, and I know that together, we can build a future where our voices are heard. But to do that, we need to win.
We’re up against big money and establishment politics, but I believe in the power of grassroots movements. Every conversation, every handshake, and every donation makes a difference.
If you’re ready to send real leadership to Washington, chip in $9 today and help us keep up the momentum, we only have 35 days until elections, and we have a lot of work to do. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gay2025klemail]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
Express Donate: $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gay2025klemail?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=express_lane_5]
Express Donate: $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gay2025klemail?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=express_lane_10]
Express Donate: $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gay2025klemail?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=express_lane_25]
Express Donate: $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gay2025klemail?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=express_lane_100]
Or, donate another amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gay2025klemail?express_lane_other=true&refcode=express_lane_other]
Northwest Florida deserves a representative who fights for them. Let’s make it happen.
With gratitude,
Gay Valimont
Candidate for Congress, FL-1
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP59/1/107746/images/GaySignoff.png]
Paid For By Gay Valimont
Gay Valimont For Congress
PO Box 1258
Gulf Breeze, FL 32562
United States

D.O.G.E. isn’t just about cutting WASTE, FRAUD, & ABUSE — it’s about returning control of the government back to YOU THE PEOPLE!

That’s why it’s so important to me that my new initiative has your stamp of approval.

name: Senator Jennifer Boysko
subject: Boysko Bulletin: Wrapped - 2025 General Assembly Session
body:
| | |
---
**Boysko Bulletin:****Wrapped - 2025 General Assembly Session**
---
Dear Peter, Last Saturday, we cast our final votes for the 2025 General Assembly Session and returned home to our districts. We return to Richmond on April 2nd to vote on the Governor’s vetoes and amendments. Over the past weeks, the House and Senate Finance Committees worked to reconcile their budgets, creating a [conference report](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103058129/531083367/-1239898710?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmFlZWM2MTQtMDJmNy1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5M2I1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=68f_YoVEcR4_V_1tB20ivFCJkIHw1fK0MlHC4STCbHc=&emci=a37d44a5-afec-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=baeec614-02f7-ef11-90cb-0022482a93b5&ceid=22486556) which we voted to approve. See the Commonwealth Institute comparison of the Governor’s budget and the conference budget [here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103058132/531083372/750487419?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmFlZWM2MTQtMDJmNy1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5M2I1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=68f_YoVEcR4_V_1tB20ivFCJkIHw1fK0MlHC4STCbHc=&emci=a37d44a5-afec-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=baeec614-02f7-ef11-90cb-0022482a93b5&ceid=22486556). The conference budget includes several of my budget requests including $3.3 million for WMATA and $222,915,021 to raise the Support Cap for public school staff. And we adopted my budget amendment to restore funding of abortion for fetal anomalies for people with limited financial resources. We also removed the language restricting application of the Earned Sentence Credit program in the Department of Corrections. We voted to increase tax fairness, strengthen public schools and foster employment opportunities. The conference budget increases and extends the state’s refundable Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) from 15% to 20% of the federal credit, which will make our tax code more progressive and fairer. This tax policy choice will mean a married couple with two kids making $30,000 a year will get an additional $300+ back during tax filing season compared to the 15% option, totaling more than $1,000. The conference budget continues recent historic investments made by state lawmakers in public education. As I mentioned above, we have finally lifted the arbitrary cap on state funding for student support positions, which covers the state share of the cost of an additional 4,725 support staff to help students succeed in the classroom. For Fairfax County, that could support an additional 688 staff members at a cost of $20,273,915. Additionally, we added an additional $52.8 million in flexible funds for localities to be able to better support students with disabilities by paying for critical supports like learning aides and assistive technology. In order to help build an economy that works for everyone, we voted to keep Virginia on a path to a $15 an hour minimum wage by 2027. The budget also includes funding for paid family and medical leave for workers that have previously not had access to paid leave - you know that I’ve been fighting for that since 2019. These proposals will help increase economic opportunity and allow people to take paid time off to care for themselves and their loved ones. I am proud of the legislation we passed, including my 13 bills that passed the General Assembly. Twelve will be sent to the Governor for his signature or amendment or veto. You can ask the Governor to support the legislation passed by the General Assembly by reaching out via his [website](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103058134/531083377/1263617055?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmFlZWM2MTQtMDJmNy1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5M2I1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=68f_YoVEcR4_V_1tB20ivFCJkIHw1fK0MlHC4STCbHc=&emci=a37d44a5-afec-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=baeec614-02f7-ef11-90cb-0022482a93b5&ceid=22486556). The Constitutional Amendment for Reproductive Freedom is a resolution and is not sent to the Governor. My bill for campaign finance reform is 12 years in the making - initiated by Delegate Simon. With bipartisan support, we are ensuring that campaign funds will not be used for personal enrichment. I am hopeful that the Governor will sign this legislation. I have continued to fight for Virginians with disabilities, passing legislation providing an affirmative defense or reduced penalty for a neurocognitive disorder or intellectual or developmental disability. We should not criminalize the behavior of people who are experiencing a crisis or cannot process what is being asked of them. This legislation passed with bipartisan support, we’ve made changes as requested by law enforcement and prosecutors. I am hopeful that the Governor will sign this legislation - disability impacts families from all political parties. I am proud of passing legislation that would narrow the wage gap for women and minorities. Research shows that including wage/salary ranges with job postings and not using salary history when hiring narrows the wage gap. The Governor should sign this legislation - businesses around the world include the wage/salary range on job postings. Virginia employers are smart and this is an easy way to avoid discriminating against women and minorities in hiring. The General Assembly passed my safe storage of firearms legislation again this year - it requires locking up firearms when there is a minor or other person not allowed contact with firearms in the home. We continued to see children lost to gun violence this past year when parents put their guns down after returning home. We can do better for our children and I hope that the Governor acts in their best interests. My colleagues and I approved historic legislation to expand collective bargaining rights for public service workers in Virginia. I was proud to serve as Chief Copatron on this year’s bill. I have carried this legislation for years. In 2020 legislation that Senator Saslaw and I sponsored allowed localities to enter into collective bargaining agreements so that teachers can negotiate for things like reduced class sizes and school supplies, while firefighters can negotiate for continuous training and better equipment. The legislation we passed this session will remove the prohibition on collective bargaining for virtually all government workers, reduce costly staff turnovers and ensure we have the best public sector workforce to meet the everyday needs of the constituents I serve. I am back home in NOVA, looking forward to holding town halls and meeting with you all. My office will continue its constituent services work - I’ve added sections to this newsletter for federal workers and immigrants. I am deeply disturbed about the disrespect of and financial distress caused by the actions of the current federal Administration. Your commitment to the mission of your offices has not gone unnoticed and we will be worse off without your contributions. I expect to return to Richmond for a special session this year to deal with the impacts of the federal layoffs and funding cuts on our Commonwealth. As for the cruel policies impacting immigrants, you know where I stand, we are better when our community is welcoming, vibrant and diverse. Kindest regards,

**Legislative Updates** **Senator Boysko’s 2025 Legislative Slate** **SJ247 Constitutional amendment; fundamental right to reproductive freedom (first reference):** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1002 Campaign finance; prohibited personal use of campaign funds; complaints, hearings, civil penalty, and advisory opinions:** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1004 Trailer franchise requirements; applicability to certain trailers** : Passed the General Assembly! **SB1005 Services for sexual assault patients; provision of information for sexual assault patients; Task Force on Services for Survivors of Sexual Assault; work group; report** : Passed the General Assembly! **SB1013 Affirmative defense or reduced penalty for a neurocognitive disorder or intellectual or developmental disability** : Passed the General Assembly! **SB1122 Paid family and medical leave insurance program; notice requirements; civil action** : Passed the Senate; failed in the House after it was brought to the Floor as a revenue bill requiring more than a simple majority vote. While frustrating, I am glad that Delegate Sewell's identical bill passed in both the House and the Senate. **SB1123 Extreme Weather Relief Act established:** passed by in the Senate Agriculture, Conservation and Natural Resources Committee with a letter for further study **SB1124 Board of Education; driver education programs; computer-based driver education courses; requirements** : Passed the General Assembly! **SB1125 Department of Wildlife Resources; propagation of wildlife without a permit unlawful; premature separation; hybridization:** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1126 Students with individualized education programs or Section 504 Plans; emergency protocol and guide:** killed in the full Senate Education and Health Committee although I am pleased that Delegate Cohen’s version passed the General Assembly **SB1127 Animal testing facilities; public notification:** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1129 National Teacher Certification Incentive Reward Program and Fund; eligibility; incentive grant awards:** reported from the Senate Education and Health Committee and killed in the Senate Finance and Appropriations Committee. **SB1132 Prohibiting employer seeking wage or salary history of prospective employees; wage or salary range transparency; cause of action:** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1133 Restaurants and catering operations; notice of food containing pork products; civil penalty:** Passed the Senate; killed in the House Health Subcommittee of the Committee on Health and Human Services **SB1134 Storage of firearms in a residence where a minor or person prohibited from possessing a firearm is present; penalty:** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1135 Board of Pharmacy; regulation of COMP360:** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1136 Local anti-rent gouging authority; civil penalty:** killed by the Senate General Laws Committee **SB1137 Office of Data Governance and Analytics; oversight; Chief Data Officer; duties:** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1237 Department of Medical Assistance Services; appeals of agency determinations (Admin Bill):** Passed the General Assembly! **SB1483 Regulated hemp product retail facility registration; fee:** passed the Senate; killed by the Agriculture Subcommittee of the House Agriculture, Chesapeake and Natural Resources Committee

| | **Constituent Services** **Federal Workers and Others Searching for Work** In 2024, there were more than 28,000 federal jobs in Fairfax County, with 78,800 Fairfax County residents working for the federal government. Serious attempts to move the federal government out of our region and to continue with layoffs will have a significant impact on our economy. Of course, the impacts are most serious for the employees who have been threatened with layoffs or fired. I am so sorry for what is happening. I’ve included resources in this newsletter and you are welcome to email me. Federal workers laid off or furloughed by the new federal administration are encouraged to reach out to their federal legislators - [Senator Warner](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103058136/531083379/1384386641?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmFlZWM2MTQtMDJmNy1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5M2I1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=68f_YoVEcR4_V_1tB20ivFCJkIHw1fK0MlHC4STCbHc=&emci=a37d44a5-afec-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=baeec614-02f7-ef11-90cb-0022482a93b5&ceid=22486556), [Senator Kaine](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103058137/531083381/1707660458?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmFlZWM2MTQtMDJmNy1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5M2I1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=68f_YoVEcR4_V_1tB20ivFCJkIHw1fK0MlHC4STCbHc=&emci=a37d44a5-afec-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=baeec614-02f7-ef11-90cb-0022482a93b5&ceid=22486556) and your [congressional representative](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103058138/531083382/-591754513?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmFlZWM2MTQtMDJmNy1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5M2I1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=68f_YoVEcR4_V_1tB20ivFCJkIHw1fK0MlHC4STCbHc=&emci=a37d44a5-afec-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=baeec614-02f7-ef11-90cb-0022482a93b5&ceid=22486556). **Addiction Recovery** As a member of the [Addiction and Recovery Council](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103058157/531083400/534806463?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmFlZWM2MTQtMDJmNy1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5M2I1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=68f_YoVEcR4_V_1tB20ivFCJkIHw1fK0MlHC4STCbHc=&emci=a37d44a5-afec-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=baeec614-02f7-ef11-90cb-0022482a93b5&ceid=22486556), we work together to ensure that people have the resources they need to identify and get help for addiction recovery. Addiction is a life threatening problem for too many Virginians. Please reach out if you need help for yourself or a loved one. The [Substance Use Prevention program](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103058158/531083401/1222458286?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmFlZWM2MTQtMDJmNy1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5M2I1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=68f_YoVEcR4_V_1tB20ivFCJkIHw1fK0MlHC4STCbHc=&emci=a37d44a5-afec-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=baeec614-02f7-ef11-90cb-0022482a93b5&ceid=22486556), a partnership between the Fairfax-Falls Church Community Services Board, Fairfax County Public Schools (FCPS) and Juvenile and Domestic Relations District Court's Diversion Unit, works to provide substance use prevention, assessments and treatment to adolescents who are at risk of or who are actively using substances. Youth substance use can be overcome. Early detection and treatment are key to recovery – support is a phone call away. For additional information, please call the Substance Use Disorder Prevention program case manager at 703-866-2121. **Support for Our Immigrant Community Members** I will continue to work to ensure that our communities are welcoming, diverse and provide opportunities for all people to live with dignity and safety. Working together, we can build good lives for our families and our communities. The news of ICE sweeps is distressing. We can help by sharing information with our neighbors regarding their rights. We can volunteer to help people fill out forms and warn people to avoid overpaying for a notary (it can be free and should not be more than $10). We can [print and share the “red cards”](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103058159/531083402/-2121718050?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmFlZWM2MTQtMDJmNy1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5M2I1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=68f_YoVEcR4_V_1tB20ivFCJkIHw1fK0MlHC4STCbHc=&emci=a37d44a5-afec-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=baeec614-02f7-ef11-90cb-0022482a93b5&ceid=22486556) which contain directions for handling interaction with law enforcement in several languages. Please share this information from the ACLU, available in multiple languages: [“Know Your Rights.”](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103058162/531083404/-939671523?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmFlZWM2MTQtMDJmNy1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5M2I1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=68f_YoVEcR4_V_1tB20ivFCJkIHw1fK0MlHC4STCbHc=&emci=a37d44a5-afec-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=baeec614-02f7-ef11-90cb-0022482a93b5&ceid=22486556) We are also working with the Legal Aid Justice Center which has asked that we share their [Rapid Response Toolkit](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103058163/531083406/1464008473?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmFlZWM2MTQtMDJmNy1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5M2I1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=68f_YoVEcR4_V_1tB20ivFCJkIHw1fK0MlHC4STCbHc=&emci=a37d44a5-afec-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=baeec614-02f7-ef11-90cb-0022482a93b5&ceid=22486556). Fairfax County has a [website page for immigrants](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103058164/531083407/54767655?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmFlZWM2MTQtMDJmNy1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5M2I1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=68f_YoVEcR4_V_1tB20ivFCJkIHw1fK0MlHC4STCbHc=&emci=a37d44a5-afec-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=baeec614-02f7-ef11-90cb-0022482a93b5&ceid=22486556). Fairfax County Public Schools also has a page: [Resources for Immigrant Families; Safe, Welcoming, and Inclusive Schools](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103058165/531083408/-734194335?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmFlZWM2MTQtMDJmNy1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5M2I1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=68f_YoVEcR4_V_1tB20ivFCJkIHw1fK0MlHC4STCbHc=&emci=a37d44a5-afec-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=baeec614-02f7-ef11-90cb-0022482a93b5&ceid=22486556). Click on ["Support SPARC"](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103058169/531083412/-1692866987?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDI2MjQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmFlZWM2MTQtMDJmNy1lZjExLTkwY2ItMDAyMjQ4MmE5M2I1IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=68f_YoVEcR4_V_1tB20ivFCJkIHw1fK0MlHC4STCbHc=&emci=a37d44a5-afec-ef11-90cb-0022482a94f4&emdi=baeec614-02f7-ef11-90cb-0022482a93b5&ceid=22486556) to make a monetary donation. clean_body: | | | Boysko Bulletin:Wrapped - 2025 General Assembly Session Dear Peter, Last Saturday, we cast our final votes for the 2025 General Assembly Session and returned home to our districts. We return to Richmond on April 2nd to vote on the Governor’s vetoes and amendments. Over the past weeks, the House and Senate Finance Committees worked to reconcile their budgets, creating a which we voted to approve. See the Commonwealth Institute comparison of the Governor’s budget and the conference budget . The conference budget includes several of my budget requests including $3.3 million for WMATA and $222,915,021 to raise the Support Cap for public school staff. And we adopted my budget amendment to restore funding of abortion for fetal anomalies for people with limited financial resources. We also removed the language restricting application of the Earned Sentence Credit program in the Department of Corrections. We voted to increase tax fairness, strengthen public schools and foster employment opportunities. The conference budget increases and extends the state’s refundable Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) from 15% to 20% of the federal credit, which will make our tax code more progressive and fairer. This tax policy choice will mean a married couple with two kids making $30,000 a year will get an additional $300+ back during tax filing season compared to the 15% option, totaling more than $1,000. The conference budget continues recent historic investments made by state lawmakers in public education. As I mentioned above, we have finally lifted the arbitrary cap on state funding for student support positions, which covers the state share of the cost of an additional 4,725 support staff to help students succeed in the classroom. For Fairfax County, that could support an additional 688 staff members at a cost of $20,273,915. Additionally, we added an additional $52.8 million in flexible funds for localities to be able to better support students with disabilities by paying for critical supports like learning aides and assistive technology. In order to help build an economy that works for everyone, we voted to keep Virginia on a path to a $15 an hour minimum wage by 2027. The budget also includes funding for paid family and medical leave for workers that have previously not had access to paid leave - you know that I’ve been fighting for that since 2019. These proposals will help increase economic opportunity and allow people to take paid time off to care for themselves and their loved ones. I am proud of the legislation we passed, including my 13 bills that passed the General Assembly. Twelve will be sent to the Governor for his signature or amendment or veto. You can ask the Governor to support the legislation passed by the General Assembly by reaching out via his . The Constitutional Amendment for Reproductive Freedom is a resolution and is not sent to the Governor. My bill for campaign finance reform is 12 years in the making - initiated by Delegate Simon. With bipartisan support, we are ensuring that campaign funds will not be used for personal enrichment. I am hopeful that the Governor will sign this legislation. I have continued to fight for Virginians with disabilities, passing legislation providing an affirmative defense or reduced penalty for a neurocognitive disorder or intellectual or developmental disability. We should not criminalize the behavior of people who are experiencing a crisis or cannot process what is being asked of them. This legislation passed with bipartisan support, we’ve made changes as requested by law enforcement and prosecutors. I am hopeful that the Governor will sign this legislation - disability impacts families from all political parties. I am proud of passing legislation that would narrow the wage gap for women and minorities. Research shows that including wage/salary ranges with job postings and not using salary history when hiring narrows the wage gap. The Governor should sign this legislation - businesses around the world include the wage/salary range on job postings. Virginia employers are smart and this is an easy way to avoid discriminating against women and minorities in hiring. The General Assembly passed my safe storage of firearms legislation again this year - it requires locking up firearms when there is a minor or other person not allowed contact with firearms in the home. We continued to see children lost to gun violence this past year when parents put their guns down after returning home. We can do better for our children and I hope that the Governor acts in their best interests. My colleagues and I approved historic legislation to expand collective bargaining rights for public service workers in Virginia. I was proud to serve as Chief Copatron on this year’s bill. I have carried this legislation for years. In 2020 legislation that Senator Saslaw and I sponsored allowed localities to enter into collective bargaining agreements so that teachers can negotiate for things like reduced class sizes and school supplies, while firefighters can negotiate for continuous training and better equipment. The legislation we passed this session will remove the prohibition on collective bargaining for virtually all government workers, reduce costly staff turnovers and ensure we have the best public sector workforce to meet the everyday needs of the constituents I serve. I am back home in NOVA, looking forward to holding town halls and meeting with you all. My office will continue its constituent services work - I’ve added sections to this newsletter for federal workers and immigrants. I am deeply disturbed about the disrespect of and financial distress caused by the actions of the current federal Administration. Your commitment to the mission of your offices has not gone unnoticed and we will be worse off without your contributions. I expect to return to Richmond for a special session this year to deal with the impacts of the federal layoffs and funding cuts on our Commonwealth. As for the cruel policies impacting immigrants, you know where I stand, we are better when our community is welcoming, vibrant and diverse. Kindest regards, Jennifer B. BoyskoMember, Senate of Virginia Representing the newly created 38th Senate District making up the northernmost portions of Fairfax County (Great Falls, Herndon, McLean, Reston, WolfTrap, parts of unincorporated Vienna and a slice of Falls Church) PS If you would like to support my work, please visit =. Photo Gallery At the end of the short 45 day session, 13 of 20 of my bills passed and 12 will go to the Governor to sign or veto. The Constitutional Amendment for Reproductive Freedom is a resolution that does not go to the Governor. If you visited my office in Richmond, you would have seen this board where we tracked our bills. During our last week of Session, I received flowers from the No Fairfax Casino Coalition. I am thankful for all the love and support I received from constituents who stood with me in opposition to the legislation targeting our community for a casino. While my bill requiring labeling pork on menus and food buffets failed in the House of Delegates, the pages were more successful at their mock session. Congratulations to our future legislators who understand the importance of religious dietary restrictions, food allergies, and health preferences. Press Conference to celebrate passing campaign finance legislation with Delegates Cherry (R-Colonial Heights), Simon (D-Falls Church), Convirs-Fowler (D-Virginia Beach), and Cole (D-Fredericksburg). Discussing legislative priorities with a group of Virginia Young Democrats from across the state. Meeting with members of the Metro Richmond Area Young Democrats, including my former session aide Randy Sprinkle and my current session aide Gillie Cuda. Legislative Updates Senator Boysko’s 2025 Legislative Slate SJ247 Constitutional amendment; fundamental right to reproductive freedom (first reference): Passed the General Assembly! SB1002 Campaign finance; prohibited personal use of campaign funds; complaints, hearings, civil penalty, and advisory opinions: Passed the General Assembly! SB1004 Trailer franchise requirements; applicability to certain trailers : Passed the General Assembly! SB1005 Services for sexual assault patients; provision of information for sexual assault patients; Task Force on Services for Survivors of Sexual Assault; work group; report : Passed the General Assembly! SB1013 Affirmative defense or reduced penalty for a neurocognitive disorder or intellectual or developmental disability : Passed the General Assembly! SB1122 Paid family and medical leave insurance program; notice requirements; civil action : Passed the Senate; failed in the House after it was brought to the Floor as a revenue bill requiring more than a simple majority vote. While frustrating, I am glad that Delegate Sewell's identical bill passed in both the House and the Senate. SB1123 Extreme Weather Relief Act established: passed by in the Senate Agriculture, Conservation and Natural Resources Committee with a letter for further study SB1124 Board of Education; driver education programs; computer-based driver education courses; requirements : Passed the General Assembly! SB1125 Department of Wildlife Resources; propagation of wildlife without a permit unlawful; premature separation; hybridization: Passed the General Assembly! SB1126 Students with individualized education programs or Section 504 Plans; emergency protocol and guide: killed in the full Senate Education and Health Committee although I am pleased that Delegate Cohen’s version passed the General Assembly SB1127 Animal testing facilities; public notification: Passed the General Assembly! SB1129 National Teacher Certification Incentive Reward Program and Fund; eligibility; incentive grant awards: reported from the Senate Education and Health Committee and killed in the Senate Finance and Appropriations Committee. SB1132 Prohibiting employer seeking wage or salary history of prospective employees; wage or salary range transparency; cause of action: Passed the General Assembly! SB1133 Restaurants and catering operations; notice of food containing pork products; civil penalty: Passed the Senate; killed in the House Health Subcommittee of the Committee on Health and Human Services SB1134 Storage of firearms in a residence where a minor or person prohibited from possessing a firearm is present; penalty: Passed the General Assembly! SB1135 Board of Pharmacy; regulation of COMP360: Passed the General Assembly! SB1136 Local anti-rent gouging authority; civil penalty: killed by the Senate General Laws Committee SB1137 Office of Data Governance and Analytics; oversight; Chief Data Officer; duties: Passed the General Assembly! SB1237 Department of Medical Assistance Services; appeals of agency determinations (Admin Bill): Passed the General Assembly! SB1483 Regulated hemp product retail facility registration; fee: passed the Senate; killed by the Agriculture Subcommittee of the House Agriculture, Chesapeake and Natural Resources Committee | | Constituent Services Federal Workers and Others Searching for Work In 2024, there were more than 28,000 federal jobs in Fairfax County, with 78,800 Fairfax County residents working for the federal government. Serious attempts to move the federal government out of our region and to continue with layoffs will have a significant impact on our economy. Of course, the impacts are most serious for the employees who have been threatened with layoffs or fired. I am so sorry for what is happening. I’ve included resources in this newsletter and you are welcome to email me. Federal workers laid off or furloughed by the new federal administration are encouraged to reach out to their federal legislators - , and your . Fairfax County has created a webpage with a variety of resources that can help former federal employees, contractors and anyone else affected by job loss. Services include searching and preparing for a new job with listings available through the County and a database maintained by the Fairfax County Economic Development Authority. Financial help is available for many health and human service programs, taxes and pet-related expenses. A link to the new webpage is included below: . The Fairfax County Economic Development Authority, with funding from the Fairfax County Board of Supervisors, has introduced a one-stop resource for connecting with hundreds of employers actively hiring in our region: . You can watch their . They have 90,000 jobs posted. For those eligible for unemployment benefits, the Virginia Employment Commission (VEC) requires that people receiving unemployment benefits show proof of job searches each week. I encourage you to seek support for finding a job from . They help identify training programs, job opportunities, and help with resumes, emails, and interviewing skills. My constituents report good results working with VCW. . is a initiative. The site includes suggestions for federal workers seeking to rewrite their resumes for the private sector, plus region-by-region links to major employers’ corporate career pages and links to region-specific job searches on Indeed and LinkedIn. Next Wednesday, March 5th, they are sponsoring a statewide virtual hiring event. Sign up . Help Paying Bills If you are struggling to pay bills, please visit Virginia’s ** to figure out if you qualify for assistance. You can also reach out to your county - your local government will review your financial situation and connect you with resources. In Fairfax County, contact . are additional resources for food. If you have lost your health insurance, please visit to figure out if you are eligible for Medicaid or for help with Affordable Care Act insurance. Reach Out for Mental Health Support The is available nationwide. You can also find support for yourself and family members by reaching out to or. The Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration National Helpline: 1-800-662-4357 National Drug Helpline: 1-844-289-0879 National Institute of Mental Health Information Resource Center: 1-866-615-6464 Partnership for Drug-Free Kids: 1-855-378-4373 Addiction Recovery As a member of the , we work together to ensure that people have the resources they need to identify and get help for addiction recovery. Addiction is a life threatening problem for too many Virginians. Please reach out if you need help for yourself or a loved one. The , a partnership between the Fairfax-Falls Church Community Services Board, Fairfax County Public Schools (FCPS) and Juvenile and Domestic Relations District Court's Diversion Unit, works to provide substance use prevention, assessments and treatment to adolescents who are at risk of or who are actively using substances. Youth substance use can be overcome. Early detection and treatment are key to recovery – support is a phone call away. For additional information, please call the Substance Use Disorder Prevention program case manager at 703-866-2121. Support for Our Immigrant Community Members I will continue to work to ensure that our communities are welcoming, diverse and provide opportunities for all people to live with dignity and safety. Working together, we can build good lives for our families and our communities. The news of ICE sweeps is distressing. We can help by sharing information with our neighbors regarding their rights. We can volunteer to help people fill out forms and warn people to avoid overpaying for a notary (it can be free and should not be more than $10). We can which contain directions for handling interaction with law enforcement in several languages. Please share this information from the ACLU, available in multiple languages: We are also working with the Legal Aid Justice Center which has asked that we share their . Fairfax County has a . Fairfax County Public Schools also has a page: . Our community is better when we embrace our diversity and keep each other safe! Opportunities Apply for the Sorensen Institute for Political Leadership High School Leaders Program The University of Virginia is now accepting applications for its Sorensen Institute High School Leaders Program. Participants learn how Virginia government, both state and local, functions, how public policy is shaped and how to communicate effectively. This program prepares future leaders for ethical and responsible civic engagement and public service in the Commonwealth of Virginia. Applications for this in-person summer program, July 12 - 26, 2025, are due by March 17, 2025 with supplemental materials due by March 21, 2025. Tuition details and financial aid applications are available . Give Back provides an environment where young adults with severe disabilities can build relationships and community. District 38 locations include the Cathy Hudgins Center at Southgate and the Old Firehouse Teen Center in McLean. The Sully Community Center in Herndon is nearby. Click on to make a monetary donation. SPARC also needs caring people to invest their time and expertise in order to offer programs that give SPARC participants a chance to enjoy a fuller life. Contact Executive Director Debi Alexander at 571-407-1807 if you can provide scholarships, in-kind donations including equipment, materials, access to unique activities, or other products and services supporting SPARC's mission. In the News Keep in Touch** I am back in District 38 with my Chief of Staff Karen Harrison, and aides, Kathy Shupe, Gillie Cuda, and Juliet Morro. I am very grateful for the hard work of my staff during the session, especially our wonderful Administrative Assistant Sandi Bassett and our interns: Ava Kegler from Hollins University, and Olga Rokhlenko and Jackson Sanders-Bussey from the University of Richmond. For constituent services and to schedule meetings with your community group, please call (703) 437-0086 or email Senator Boysko@senate.virginia.gov. Peter, we’ve just received breaking news out of Florida:
Governor Ron DeSantis has officially called on the Secretary of State to schedule TWO special elections on a RAPIDLY accelerated timeline.
To put it simply: Democrats now have a limited time to prepare to win TWO MORE HOUSE SEATS being vacated by Trump cabinet appointments
These special elections will determine the future of FL-01 , vacated by Matt Gaetz (reportedly stepping up as Trump’s pick for Attorney General), and FL-06 , soon to be left by Mike Waltz (rumored to become Trump’s National Security Advisor).
Flipping these suits blue IS possible - but only if we start preparing NOW. Will you help us build up a warchest of funding to rush to the Democrats who run for these seats? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?refcode=111724_1]

name: Hillsdale College via GOP Presidential
subject: What is our purpose?
body:
Reason and speech are gifts from God that enable us to think about God and to worship Him.

This sponsored message from GOP Presidential is on behalf of Hillsdale College
[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zt4vvg0nhqumkft0g5h2ct69becjtg66c4ul11dg_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj2c1h2bs) Dear Fellow American, I hope your day is going well. I’m writing to let you know about something I think you’ll find useful in your learning. Reason and speech are gifts from God that enable us to think about God and to worship Him. [](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zd6l827tdsggl3kqeghucalflmvpihmsup0aub80_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj2c1h2bs) One of the chief aims of education at Hillsdale College is to understand what kind of being we are, as well as our purpose. That, in turn, makes it possible to begin to understand our relationship to God.

The study of God, also known as “theology,” has had a profound impact on Western civilization, stretching back thousands of years to the ancient Hebrews. And that’s what Hillsdale’s **“Theology 101”** online course is about—the development of theology from the Old Testament through the twentieth century.

**“Theology 101”** has been a popular course, so I’m pleased to let you know that it is [**now available for you to own on DVD**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z113hn37u9ojja4lg3lca48n24d4f1mn773lk7po_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj2c1h2bs). You can add it to your home library or give it as a gift to a friend or relative.

We do have a limited number of sets in stock, though—and they go fast—so don’t delay in getting your copy of **“Theology 101”** on DVD. Secure yours right now at: **[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/support-hillsdales-online-courses-theology-101-dvd](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zjhatrok4i51394qe7n1e69ds8mai3qojaunlq5g_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj2c1h2bs)** Best regards,

Jeremiah Regan, Ph.D.
Executive Director of Online Learning
Hillsdale College
---

GOP Presidential
---
_PO Box 2221 Fort Collins, CO 80522_
[unsubscribe](http://e.goppresidential.com/prefs/9z2z7nh59ps8non22ivhttriq52nj5r27kl3lp54d9o) PO Box 2221 Fort Collins, CO 80522

name: Vivek Ramaswamy
subject: I wrote you a note
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VIVEK RAMASWAMY FOR OHIO
body:
I’d like to explain why if you’ll indulge me for a moment.

| |
---
| | | Hi, it's Vivek Ramaswamy.

I wanted you to hear this directly from me: _I JUST announced my candidacy for Governor of Ohio. _
---
| [](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zp5gnr2b1b6g949ngq6nqmp6rkvfn7jmivj1fb10_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs)
---
**[In a moment, I'm going to ask you to become a Founding Donor to this historic campaign - but first, I'd like to explain why if you'll indulge me for a moment.](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zvqi3uujtclgc18g71skq4lk4249h9us91chifeo_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs "In a moment, I’m going to ask you to become a Founding Donor to this historic campaign – but first, I’d like to explain why if you’ll indulge me for a moment.")**

---
** WHY RUN NOW? **
---
President Trump winning re-election in November was not just another political victory - it was a seismic realignment for our country. He won every swing state. He improved his vote share of minorities and young voters. We won the House AND Senate. And heck, he even won the popular vote.

---
| [](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zc3ampf9hfhda21ddjjjfv8qhb5n4u8ki6ldum4o_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs)
---
It was the honor of a lifetime to rally voters in battleground states, campaign on college campuses to turn out Gen-Z for Trump, and promote his America First vision at rally after rally.

---
| [](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zsu1r16fdpq2hmahfonf81biot67vof024muv9tg_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs)
---
Right now, we have a once-in-a-generation chance to make real, lasting change to the country we know and love. It's an opportunity to not just ticker around the edges of the Federal Government, but to upend it completely.

This is our moment to GUT the managerial bureaucracy, FIRE anyone connected to the weaponization of our own government against us, SLASH the size and scope of the administrative state, and usher in the GOLDEN AGE for America!

But the Deep State is already putting up the fight of its life. They're resisting change at every turn. Make no mistake - we can't afford to squander this historic opportunity. That's why President Trump needs trusted partnerships at the state level RIGHT NOW!

---
** WHY OHIO? **
---
This one's easy. I was born and raised here in the Buckeye State and graduated from St. Xavier - a Catholic high school in Cincinnati. My wife, Apporva, and I live outside Columbus, raising our two boys here. I've started multiple businesses in Ohio. I've also known JD Vance since college and campaigned all over the state for our excellent new Senator, Bernie Moreno.
---
| [](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zd6eg1pva0n518pubde0p9c7adir3e0e2ngetaog_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs)
---
The people here are honest, have steel in their spines, and embody the City on a Hill spirit that our country was founded upon. [As Governor, I'd be honored to represent them and the values they stand for.](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2za6arnqqd61act0dulcrhp3vtkqamo5llmfrk8l8_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs "As Governor, I’d be honored to represent them and the values they stand for.")
---
| [](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2znva04q2ro1qekqgtufumc6ki6g490giblg1kc8o_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs)
---
** WHY GOVERNOR? **
---
Having traveled across the country, met with hundreds of politicians, ran multiple businesses, and seen how deals really get cut in The Swamp, I have never been more certain that REAL change only gets done with buy-in at the executive level.

---
| [](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zc4q79v5uiqf3em7jsjeauvo8e7a3htavj6vf49o_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs)
---
We saw up close and personal the failure of Democrat rule with Joe Biden in control of the Federal Government. We also see Democrat governors like Gavin Newsom and Kathy Hochul drive their states into ruin. On the other hand, we now see President Trump act quickly and decisively to fix our country by deporting illegal criminals, moving to cut taxes, securing our border, gutting DEI, and obliterating the Deep State. True leadership can change everything.
---
| [](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z7f2lbiffqprr81tga7hsgth6im0em1v3fvncv6o_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs)
---
That's why I'm running for Governor in my home state of Ohio. I will partner with President Trump - just like I did on the campaign trail - at the state level to Make America Great Again. [I will be a change agent for our state and set an example of efficient, conservative governance for the rest of the country.](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z0jmsnumqt20hfk01ehsod196o3e7lhh4q0a44po_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs "I will be a change agent for our state and set an example of efficient, conservative governance for the rest of the country.")

So there you have it. That's the "why" behind my run for Governor.

**[It would be the honor of a lifetime to have your support now that I've officially launched my campaign. I'm humbly asking you to become a Founding Donor today with a gift of any size.](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zaohdvabkpaqjfk99c4cokm126fkmslvd7kqmjk8_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs "It would be the honor of a lifetime to have your support now that I’ve officially launched my campaign. I’m humbly asking you to become a Founding Donor today with a gift of any size.")**

---
| | [**CONTRIBUTE $4 HERE**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zg194jsfomdntv5hheaj3cee144f0slhsp3rr9r0_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs)
---
| | [**CONTRIBUTE $40 HERE**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z92om2vm38sc91c9bffve146dstrjr8ag25evb38_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs)
---
| | [**CONTRIBUTE $400 HERE**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z87r5e3839jr4sa526ohgb05c0vhi98nj8n6p958_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs)
---
| | [**CONTRIBUTE ANY AMOUNT**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zehkcvaqdlrdvsb9nt44q021h93a9j5u9uuapo0o_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs)
---

Thanks for taking a minute to read this email. I appreciate your time.

God bless you and God bless America,
Vivek Ramaswamy

---
|
---
| | PAID FOR BY VIVEK RAMASWAMY FOR OHIO
---

350 E 1st Ave Ste 100
Columbus, OH 43201

[Privacy Policy](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zuu37ffhp554js42rijfb1dbmkuq76ukm4h70n3g_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs)

Updates for Great Americans
---
_PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522_
_[**unsubscribe**](http://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zavf2i50j7mn1ievmjmncud5pd0m2joangf9m6jg_rp2ash2s8gb2sbgebjqf9tge5rrasbjklo72d1hekr76dtiesp7ad1hegqracjkmoq37djjkorrbdt5eer7ashmeop79chl6ujuadf161rn9cdhmir76pr02sqr8d5hmej3fddj4iqu8ob0ctj6ap7246h22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs)_ | _[manage data preferences](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zsglioiuj9uikaed8unv789mdacbkcled7ecvhfg_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h2bs)_

[](http://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2znd0gssmadojtsllcbqn8kcs3mlvba9r2j4f328o_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj5c5h238h5e8he49h666qbcdli6uon49fg)

name: SPECIAL ELECTION WAR CHEST
subject: Republicans in NY-21, FL-1, and FL-6 need YOU
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
Critical reinforcements are needed >>

|
---
| | | | [](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zct2ovkdmjt0kgcf7ciiotj9kbt9ejg22a9qi13o_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c1h2bs)
---

Friend,

Three crucial House seats are headed to special elections, and we need your immediate support to defend our majority. _**These seats are the key to maintaining our America First agenda, and Democrats are already mobilizing to take them from us.**_

---
| | | [- 2X SPECIAL ELECTION MATCH -](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2z70eaquqls5mj03dcnbtq9auqcq11jkjr3b41utg_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c1h2bs "Generic Button")
---
_Active for 30 minutes_

House Republican leaders are launching an emergency fundraising drive to fuel our efforts and keep these seats in Republican hands - NY-21, FL-1, and FL-6. **[And for the next 30 minutes, every dollar you give will be DOUBLED by House Republicans, so your impact will be twice as strong.](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2z7iqis2avkp3ip3rjeqjenc1h41g2spbjs5ji3tg_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c1h2bs "And for the next 30 minutes, every dollar you give will be DOUBLED by House Republicans, so your impact will be twice as strong.")**

name: Tom G. Palmer
subject: My trip to war-torn Syria. Here’s what Ifound…
body:
Did you ever think that you and the people of the Middle East face a common enemy?

|
---
| | [](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z5s7kjudt1kqvonktph89am9iofaono6toouuflg_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)
Friend,

Did you ever think that you and the people of the Middle East face a common enemy?

That enemy is pushy, aggressive, controlling, dictatorial government.

I was in Damascus, Syria, shortly after the country’s historic revolution that ended the brutal Assad dictatorship. We are witnessing a moment of profound change.

Over the past decade, millions of Syrians fled their country while millions more endured unimaginable economic hardship, brutality, and cruelty.

Now, for the first time in generations, there’s a renewed sense of hope. **That hope rests on local people building a free country founded on principles that allow people to flourish:_economic liberty, the rule of law, toleration, property rights, and free enterprise_. **

As James Madison, principal author of the United States Constitution, noted, property is the foundation of liberty and limited government:
_“As a man is said to have a right to his property, he may be equally said to have a property in his rights. Where an excess of power prevails, property of no sort is duly respected. No man is safe in his opinions, his person, his faculties, or his possessions.”_
As we speak, new pro-liberty networks and organizations inspired by Madison’s vision of liberty and limited government are already taking shape. To be successful, they need support.

Atlas Network provides training, grants, and a vital network of peers to pro-freedom organizations wherever they are, even in countries that have suffered under the heavy boot of dictatorship.

Let me be frank.

I’m asking you to support freedom around the world in some of the most tyrannical countries. I am not calling on U.S. tax dollars or armed forces to be deployed abroad. No, I want to help a global grassroots movement of private people like you and me who want freedom in their countries and who are organizing on the ground as we speak.

[Can you donate $10, $50, $100, $250, or any amount you can to support Atlas Network and our partners' fight for freedom around the world? I’m looking for 100 new supporters to join us today. Please click here to donate today.](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zpf4e85umtuuio4lst6p2b1tsgc3sivfoj0q32l8_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)
**[Donate](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zavk50s3go7ri1avjf49kvpne25osibsgbd4rrno_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)**
_By supporting Atlas Network, you’ll get our Freedom’s Champion magazine, which will keep you up to date on the latest news from around the world about people fighting for freedom._
**Here’s how Atlas Network supports our partners around the world.**
[](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zqlt998l85alfcqji4n4q6svorp3b12c30msg2k8_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)
After nearly 45 years of working in countries where freedom is under threat, Atlas Network has refined a proven process for raising the ambitions and achievements of people and teams devoted to the principles of liberty.

Atlas Network’s unique _Coach, Compete, Celebrate_ strategic model inspires our partners to improve performance and achieve extraordinary outcomes. We coach them to become better change agents; we provide investments in their projects on a competitive basis, which drives excellence; and we celebrate each achievement, which provides inspiration for the next.

I’ve seen this process work over the decades, starting with my work behind the Iron Curtain as nations broke free from the dead hand of Communism to countries struggling to break free from the poverty caused by protectionism, interventionism, and arbitrary and lawless governments.

The fight is about ideas, but it is also a clash of strategies because those with power don’t like to give it up. Their collectivist ideologies are tools for the rulers to steal from their people, grabbing lavish lifestyles as their people suffer in poverty. Even though the Maduro dictatorship in Venezuela, the Cuban Communist Party, Putin’s dictatorship in Russia, and the Iranian ruling mullahs have superficially differing ideologies, the commonality is that the thugs that run them use the power of the state to enrich themselves to the detriment of the people who live under them.

Even the most brutal states are brittle because their subjects obey from fear rather than patriotism. When freedom-loving people realized how brittle the Syrian dictator’s grip on power was, the collapse came rapidly. The talk now is not of a return to socialism but of free markets, free trade, and free people. I am guardedly hopeful that the many people I met at the Chamber of Commerce and the Chamber of Industry and among writers, entrepreneurs, scholars, and government officers will succeed in laying a lasting foundation for a future of greater freedom, lawfulness, toleration, peace, and prosperity.

[Please join the Atlas Network today by making a gift of $10 or more.](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zu2skidh2jhijuiaml8gbu9pbfoefai35p5sqia8_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)
**[Donate](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zg7t47ntpvkdd10b43ggr8l1bnv1j6o64a7d9370_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)**
**The truth is that dictators come and go.**

What makes a difference is that the ideas of liberty are widely understood and embraced by the ordinary people in the country. That is the long war that we have to fight in the Middle East and beyond. As the American founders understood, liberty is not like power; if one person or group has power, it means that others experience not power but fear.

It’s completely different with liberty because your liberty isn’t mine, but your liberty can complement it. Liberty there strengthens liberty here, and vice versa. In each case, though, liberty must be understood and embraced. The challenge of achieving world peace and world prosperity is the challenge of achieving liberty for all.

**The principles of supply and demand and fundamental economics apply everywhere, regardless of language, faith, location, or ethnicity.**

That’s why Atlas Network is bringing together our partners to support the growing freedom movement in Syria. If Syria can join the ranks of free and flourishing nations, there is a real chance for peace in the region and beyond.

[I’d like to invite you personally to join Atlas Network and to be one of our new 100 supporters today. Please select an amount below to donate $10, $35, $100, or any amount.](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z1ea8h9jl5egvj92d0n2mic80746osl5sj76rsjo_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)
**[Donate $10](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zui6tipk43850q2pfll0kbiboehu5n2m0hl46588_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)**

[**Donate $35**](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zdamcsecmpgv2e0na1npnaj3296mallsup54ao58_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)

[**Donate $50**](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z5qi776d857h0jjr38e5bjn8qvbu3erq694q7deg_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)

[**Donate $100**](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zanj21bjqd1pp59a7b8lptiliivdl5l0ce1jvvko_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)

[**Donate $250**](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zfjn0rhud2n91qtr0mm17ev52vktnol7g6s7a1t8_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)

[**Donate $500**](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z48ti43a4ib7mfv81o3hdtb5kepqj1map5uu63s0_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)

[**Donate $1,000**](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zqhdoddjiqdpn5vufn2io84aiun3g2ck63pbrcbg_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)

[**Donate Other Amount**](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zedh0enfr5tlsn4qrfkt5c6r3kk7c89icmsad3uo_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)
Together, we can ensure that more freedom movements like Syria’s — and the courageous people behind them — know that they are never alone.

Thank you,

Dr. Tom G. Palmer
P.S. Supporting Atlas Network is one of the most meaningful ways to leave a legacy of liberty.

The work done by Atlas Network and our partners affects billions of people worldwide.

Plus, your donation dollars go further in countries where authoritarian governments restrict free enterprise and individual rights. A free, just, prosperous, and peaceful world is in the interest of all.

[If you’re interested in getting the best return on your investment in improving the state of the world, then I would ask you to please donate to Atlas Network and help us provide the tools, the connections, and the ideas that make life better for everyone: liberty, limited government, toleration, and free enterprise.](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z10dipc3uriq6fd9s9k1s5h8r754oef1v08s0e98_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)
**[Donate](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z11799rsqf333f9dudo136v6lee1ui75h90jqtag_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)**

_The Atlas Economic Research Foundation is registered as a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization and does business under its trade name, Atlas Network. All contributions are tax-deductible to the extent permitted by law. The Atlas Economic Research Foundation’s tax identification number is 94-2763845._
---

Your America
---
_PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522_
_[**unsubscribe**](http://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zpt56iu29ab27kc31d1f0jiobru1ptonuf612ooo_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h238h5e8he485he5o75pt7kuo72ttle5pq9ttle9q3bd9ieir38ct3mkrnctt5eer7ashmeop79chl6ujuadf161rn9cdhmir76pr02sqr8d5hmej3fddj4iqu8ob0ctj66oj24no)_ | _[manage data preferences](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z4ukck7qenjg4opba3g7naq8s8agramkclmgutvg_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj7c5h2bs)_

body:

name: Hillsdale College via ICYMI News
subject: Participate in our national CRT Survey
body:
Now, CRT infiltrates our schools and workplaces.

This sponsored message from ICYMI News is on behalf of Hillsdale College
| | | [](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z9fqcf38i71or8n2t4pdf19r4hp1jruppgfuqeto_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj3cph2bs)
---
| | Dear Fellow American,

[**Hillsdale College has selected you to participate in its National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory (CRT).**](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z0o9jbvnl48176sfts02hd3q7t52ungqmj4ijm3g_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj3cph2bs)

I'm reaching out because you're an informed patriot who cares about this country. The situation is urgent, and your response will only take a few moments.

CRT has been around for decades.

Feminist academics used to make arguments about "unpacking their invisible knapsacks of privilege." However, at the time, CRT was rejected for being "just _another_ crazy fringe academic theory."

**Today, the situation is much different ...and much worse.**

_Now_ , CRT infiltrates our schools and workplaces. It's not so laughable for you to have to sit in a meeting or seminar that explains to you your "invisible knapsack of privilege."

To speak up often means you can lose your job, or worse...

What's frightening for the direction of this country is that federal agencies have labeled some parents who oppose CRT as "domestic terrorists."

**This should NOT be happening in America.**

Hillsdale College--where I work--teaches the principles of liberty and justice that our country was founded on. And we're fighting to defend them on the battlefield of education.

**But we need YOUR help to defend our constitutional principles against CRT.** Sharing your opinion with us is one important way you can take action.

**Complete your National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory now:[https://info.hillsdale.edu/crt-survey/](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zs276i536q79etulime5bqhm33k53e41mbpsut18_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj3cph2bs)**

When you participate in our survey, you will help us gather the information needed to address CRT's threats to our liberty and find solutions that restore our constitutional principles.

When you finish taking your survey, please consider giving a generous gift.

Our NATIONAL EDUCATION CAMPAIGN--which includes _Imprimis_ , free online courses, and K-12 curriculum and school network--educates millions of citizens about our Constitution and founding principles.

We need your help expanding these initiatives. And because we do not take a single penny of taxpayer funding, even indirectly through federal and state student grants loans--all of our work is funded by informed patriots like you.

Thank you for joining us in our mission on behalf of liberty.

Warm regards,

Bill Gray, '01
Vice President
Institutional Advancement
Hillsdale College

P.S. Your participation and support are essential in our fight against CRT. Please complete the survey and consider a generous gift to help us continue our educational mission at this secure link:

[**https://info.hillsdale.edu/crt-survey/**](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zfv8bk16hsdrbdnq3sc6m5dh743t6sgjku1sv860_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6coj3cph2bs)

name: Winsome Earle-Sears
subject: My situation is in your hands, Friend.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Winsome for Governor
body:
But right now I have a few SERIOUS challenges

| | | [](http://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zmtlsehath5as1cmpe02uslah0vhqa3ahdjkg32o_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji62p36c3h2bs)
---
My situation is in your hands, Friend. I'm writing today to give you an update and to make a humble plea. _Look - this is from the bottom of my heart, so _please_ read this message to the end._ My team and I put together a historic win in 2021 to help flip all three of Virginia's highest offices red. Ever since becoming Lt. Governor, I've been logging big wins for my friends, neighbors, and every fellow Virginian. Now, I'm running for Governor so we can continue to keep up the amazing work we've been doing here in the Commonwealth and make history again. **But right now I have a few _SERIOUS_ challenges:**
---
**I was _legally_ barred from accepting even a dime in support of my campaign while I was in the Virginia legislative session.** But my Democrat opponent, Abigail Spanberger, has been fundraising _**BIG TIME**_ while I've been working hard in our capital. **Seriously** _**, a far-Left group injected $500K into Abigail Spanberger 's campaign coffers and there wasn't a thing I could do in response!**_
---
Now Spanberger has an _ENORMOUS_ fundraising advantage, Friend. Polls show this race is tied, but Spanberger has a serious cash advantage._If we don 't make up the difference, our conservative mission _will_ be cut short. _ **Today, I 'm making a plea to you as a Christian, a mother, and a Marine; _please send in any amount you can spare to support my campaign!_**
---
| | [ DONATE TO SUPPORT WINSOME >>](http://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2ze97tudk6u6kg39u7n9mrfpnh0gqumoehlhrn4do_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji62p36c3h2bs)
---
Without your support, we will fall even _further_ behind. The Democrat elitists will log a win and our Commonwealth will regress down a path to irreversible socialism!I wouldn't ask you to stay in this fight if I didn't think it was worth it. body:

Friend,

_**[Nothing is MORE important than SECURE and FAIR elections.](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2z62718gg6d25vpg5g4sp0f5t3b0p3pqqioj0oafo_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji62p36c3h2bs)**_

That's why every American citizen should need to show a government-issued ID to vote. **[PERIOD.](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2znu2hfhu97muqos3pdjolefpddtvudl6eaos1dmg_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji62p36c3h2bs) **It's the best way to protect the integrity of our elections and instill confidence in them.
2025 VOTER ID POLL

DO YOU AGREE, YOU SHOULD BE REQUIRED TO SHOW A GOVERNMENT-ISSUED ID TO VOTE?

******************************************************[YES](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zeu650r1kupuh5idstu8qk0gusi2mcj4dtih9u1g_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji62p36c3h2bs)******************************************************

Radical Left-wing Democrats OBJECT to using VOTER ID and deride it as _" racist."_

**[DEMOCRATS WANT TO ELIMINATE VOTER ID because it's one of their last tricks to rig the system in their favor.](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2z83ijmfivuqmvd7barl1ictogpgophu952iso5k8_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji62p36c3h2bs)**

We HAVE to show them Americans SUPPORT VOTER ID.

We commissioned an exclusive **[2025 Voter ID Poll](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zks25g568prdksuo4ajgej7ecakdiuqosiqaff2o_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji62p36c3h2bs) **and only chose 1 PATRIOT from every state to represent their area - and YOU were chosen from YOUR state.

body:
We MISSED our February goal- and I’m giving you 24 hours to help us reach it

|
---
| | | | [](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2z7ndpjp3r5b0smj5hg4u00laemu1ebam7rmnqktg_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji66o72cdh2bs)
---

Friend,

The last day of February has come and gone, but we missed our fundraising goal! Not good.

Please do not panic. _I can extend our deadline for**another 24 hours only**_ , but this will be our last shot for February.

**[I'll get straight to the point: can you pitch in to help House Republicans hit our extended February goal ASAP?](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zov7qc2p901sq0a9n2pilr7um0g8a5tmtv2ggvho_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji66o72cdh2bs "I’ll get straight to the point: can you pitch in to help House Republicans hit our extended February goal ASAP?")**

The Democrat party and the Radical Left activists have been raising JAW DROPPING amounts of money since our GOP trifecta took office.

They simply cannot stand to see all the success we've had. They're more fired up than ever to take back the House in 2026 and undo all the progress we've made.

We cannot let it come to that Friend.

**[Please, chip in whatever you can spare to help us hit this HUGE goal before we fail.](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zu4606ag0hh6c5a9h05pen7s2oibvuq7hlnpff08_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji66o72cdh2bs "Please, chip in whatever you can spare to help us hit this HUGE goal before we fail.")**

**[I can only extend our deadline for another 24 hours, so please act fast.](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zhugvf22go0m996tfahfkoiaa34mli9afu3lr3dg_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji66o72cdh2bs "I can only extend our deadline for another 24 hours, so please act fast.")**

name: Hillsdale College via Your America News
subject: What is our purpose?
body:
Reason and speech are gifts from God that enable us to think about God and to worship Him.

This sponsored message from Your America News is on behalf of Hillsdale College
[](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zm5go16er5nl12fpl2b6mg73iugf25sm16c8crug_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji66o72cdh2bs) Dear Fellow American, I hope your day is going well. I’m writing to let you know about something I think you’ll find useful in your learning. Reason and speech are gifts from God that enable us to think about God and to worship Him. [](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z180kmqse3paes6qtfekf0eh7cqjgn9hkh54r0a8_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji66o72cdh2bs) One of the chief aims of education at Hillsdale College is to understand what kind of being we are, as well as our purpose. That, in turn, makes it possible to begin to understand our relationship to God.

The study of God, also known as “theology,” has had a profound impact on Western civilization, stretching back thousands of years to the ancient Hebrews. And that’s what Hillsdale’s **“Theology 101”** online course is about—the development of theology from the Old Testament through the twentieth century.

**“Theology 101”** has been a popular course, so I’m pleased to let you know that it is [**now available for you to own on DVD**](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zt3cadignr3t6lkn38d0orann0bstq0ol9gqoj9g_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji66o72cdh2bs). You can add it to your home library or give it as a gift to a friend or relative.

We do have a limited number of sets in stock, though—and they go fast—so don’t delay in getting your copy of **“Theology 101”** on DVD. Secure yours right now at: **[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/support-hillsdales-online-courses-theology-101-dvd](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zd0jicc1k65mf9ob09sk09o9u0kuoqventmfc6f0_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji66o72cdh2bs)** Best regards,

Jeremiah Regan, Ph.D.
Executive Director of Online Learning
Hillsdale College
---

Your America
---
_PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522_

body:

Friend,

Radical Left-wing Democrats OBJECT to using VOTER ID and deride it as _" racist."_

**[DEMOCRATS WANT TO ELIMINATE VOTER ID because it's one of their last tricks to rig the system in their favor.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zqgt6dbgrljq2eungdkv41gbj4pkdml8amgeeml8_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji66o70clh2bs)**

name: Hillsdale College via 1600 Daily
subject: Help us understand CRT’s influence
body:
Now, CRT infiltrates our schools and workplaces.

This sponsored message from 1600 Daily is on behalf of Hillsdale College
| | | [](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z116tjce3rnm47r5816sg59tmrfldh73r7o67430_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji66o76chh2bs)
---
| | Dear Fellow American,

[**Hillsdale College has selected you to participate in its National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory (CRT).**](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zc7vd753it1qv07eh1ktg4md0mu89oi1a1flsic8_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji66o76chh2bs)

I'm reaching out because you're an informed patriot who cares about this country. The situation is urgent, and your response will only take a few moments.

CRT has been around for decades.

Feminist academics used to make arguments about "unpacking their invisible knapsacks of privilege." However, at the time, CRT was rejected for being "just _another_ crazy fringe academic theory."

**Today, the situation is much different ...and much worse.**

_Now_ , CRT infiltrates our schools and workplaces. It's not so laughable for you to have to sit in a meeting or seminar that explains to you your "invisible knapsack of privilege."

To speak up often means you can lose your job, or worse...

What's frightening for the direction of this country is that federal agencies have labeled some parents who oppose CRT as "domestic terrorists."

**This should NOT be happening in America.**

Hillsdale College--where I work--teaches the principles of liberty and justice that our country was founded on. And we're fighting to defend them on the battlefield of education.

**But we need YOUR help to defend our constitutional principles against CRT.** Sharing your opinion with us is one important way you can take action.

**Complete your National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory now:[https://info.hillsdale.edu/crt-survey/](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zldnmo5b7cssnl5inu97uq9qr64f1s95uq3rm1j0_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji66o76chh2bs)**

When you participate in our survey, you will help us gather the information needed to address CRT's threats to our liberty and find solutions that restore our constitutional principles.

When you finish taking your survey, please consider giving a generous gift.

Our NATIONAL EDUCATION CAMPAIGN--which includes _Imprimis_ , free online courses, and K-12 curriculum and school network--educates millions of citizens about our Constitution and founding principles.

We need your help expanding these initiatives. And because we do not take a single penny of taxpayer funding, even indirectly through federal and state student grants loans--all of our work is funded by informed patriots like you.

Thank you for joining us in our mission on behalf of liberty.

Warm regards,

Bill Gray, '01
Vice President
Institutional Advancement
Hillsdale College

P.S. Your participation and support are essential in our fight against CRT. Please complete the survey and consider a generous gift to help us continue our educational mission at this secure link:

[**https://info.hillsdale.edu/crt-survey/**](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zlrkqtomhk7r7e6smt094jmkd9dt03m0kebalqg8_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji66o76chh2bs)
---

name: Hillsdale College via Updates For Great Americans
subject: Share your thoughts on CRT and its influence
body:
Now, CRT infiltrates our schools and workplaces.

This sponsored message from Great America Update is on behalf of Hillsdale College
| | | [](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zcb4pfij63pbuhdmn979kpenra6c49tepuhlabg0_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6epr6cdh2bs)
---
| | Dear Fellow American,

[**Hillsdale College has selected you to participate in its National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory (CRT).**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zpns5rvei1t6lijd9lsl6ufe1i8c6gvvjppcpr68_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6epr6cdh2bs)

I'm reaching out because you're an informed patriot who cares about this country. The situation is urgent, and your response will only take a few moments.

CRT has been around for decades.

Feminist academics used to make arguments about "unpacking their invisible knapsacks of privilege." However, at the time, CRT was rejected for being "just _another_ crazy fringe academic theory."

**Today, the situation is much different ...and much worse.**

_Now_ , CRT infiltrates our schools and workplaces. It's not so laughable for you to have to sit in a meeting or seminar that explains to you your "invisible knapsack of privilege."

To speak up often means you can lose your job, or worse...

What's frightening for the direction of this country is that federal agencies have labeled some parents who oppose CRT as "domestic terrorists."

**This should NOT be happening in America.**

Hillsdale College--where I work--teaches the principles of liberty and justice that our country was founded on. And we're fighting to defend them on the battlefield of education.

**But we need YOUR help to defend our constitutional principles against CRT.** Sharing your opinion with us is one important way you can take action.

**Complete your National Opinion Survey on Critical Race Theory now:[https://info.hillsdale.edu/crt-survey/](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zq9il721db6ldlposfs18d99juc2pq3busm4qpbo_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6epr6cdh2bs)**

When you participate in our survey, you will help us gather the information needed to address CRT's threats to our liberty and find solutions that restore our constitutional principles.

When you finish taking your survey, please consider giving a generous gift.

Our NATIONAL EDUCATION CAMPAIGN--which includes _Imprimis_ , free online courses, and K-12 curriculum and school network--educates millions of citizens about our Constitution and founding principles.

We need your help expanding these initiatives. And because we do not take a single penny of taxpayer funding, even indirectly through federal and state student grants loans--all of our work is funded by informed patriots like you.

Thank you for joining us in our mission on behalf of liberty.

Warm regards,

Bill Gray, '01
Vice President
Institutional Advancement
Hillsdale College

P.S. Your participation and support are essential in our fight against CRT. Please complete the survey and consider a generous gift to help us continue our educational mission at this secure link:

[**https://info.hillsdale.edu/crt-survey/**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zn79k9ra4mvka9fn5e98nhg8ifh2qb76vcdpm7ro_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6epr6cdh2bs)
---
| |
---

Updates for Great Americans
---
_PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522_
_[**unsubscribe**](http://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z5p3lb0m01u4b8stkgp8bha3dk6p0a6dmsgvj7co_rp2ash2s8gb2sbgebjqf9tge5rrasbjklo72d1hekr76dtiesp7ad1hegqracjkmoq37djjkorrbdt5eer7ashmeop79chl6ujuadf161rn9cdhmir76pr02sqr8d5hmej3fddj4iqu8ob0ctj69pb246h22sh2s8i6eob0cji6epr6cdh2bs)_ | _[manage data preferences](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zt3n7onvmnjlkj1stdsnr9sl2l42hkvelbkv9dqo_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6epr6cdh2bs)_

[](http://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zps5u5e2tvsbltlo16hutj8rohssf5qfdig0qt00_rp22sh2s8i6eob0cji6epr6cdh238h5e8he49h666qbcdli6uon49fg)

Friend,

The last day of February has come and gone, but we missed our fundraising goal! Not good.
